title: Gooey French Chalot Tart Galette
description: A rich and indulgent French-inspired tart galette filled with caramelized chalots, gooey cheese, and a touch of sweetness.
instructions: In a large skillet, cook the sliced chalots and minced garlic over medium heat until caramelized, stirring occasionally.
In a separate bowl, mix together the grated gruyere and parmesan cheese.
Roll out the flour to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Transfer the dough to a 9-inch tart pan with a removable bottom.
Spread the cooked chalot and garlic mixture evenly over the dough, leaving a 1-inch border around the edges.
Sprinkle the gruyere-parmesan cheese mixture over the chalot mixture.
Drizzle the honey over the cheese mixture.
Fold the edges of the dough up over the filling, pressing gently to seal.
Brush the edges of the dough with the beaten eggs.
Bake the tart galette for 25-30 minutes, or until the crust is golden brown and the filling is bubbly and slightly puffed.
Remove the tart galette from the oven and let it cool for 10-15 minutes before serving.
